description: Your university rocketry team inherited a preliminary rocket design at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/competition_rocket.ork that needs significant optimization before its certification flight. The design has undersized fins causing marginal stability, an inadequate dual-deploy recovery system with undersized parachutes, and no motor installed. You must bring this design to flight-ready status by meeting all certification requirements simultaneously: stability margin of at least 1.5 calibers, ground hit velocity no greater than 6 m/s, maximum acceleration no greater than 100 m/s squared, and apogee between 400 and 500 meters. Fix the structural and recovery deficiencies, select and configure an appropriate motor from OpenRocket's database, and iteratively tune the design parameters until all requirements are met in an up-to-date simulation. Save the final design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/competition_final.ork, export the flight simulation data to /home/ga/Documents/exports/flight_data.csv, and write a design optimization report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/design_report.txt documenting your motor choice, stability margin, predicted apogee, maximum acceleration, and descent performance.
